Patch Set 5: Code-Review-1

(7 comments)

This change and the previous change in the series have too much DIY synchronization and cleverness. Maybe they're correct but it's too hard to tell. And too fragile when modified in the future.

I support removing ptlrpc_thread but I think it should be replaced by something that is obviously correct.

parents.subject: LU-4423 llite: remove 'ptlrpc_thread usage' for sai_agl_thread

commit.subject: LU-4423 llite: change sai_thread to sai_task
commit.message:
LU-4423 llite: change sai_thread to sai_task

Rather than allocating a ptlrpc_thread for the stat-ahead thread,
just use the task_struct provided by kthreads directly.

As nothing ever waits for the sai_task, it must call do_exit() directly
rather than simply return from the function.
Also it cannot use kthread_should_stop() to know when to stop.

There is one caller which can ask it to stop so we need a simple
signaling mechanism.  I've chosen to set ->sai_task to NULL when the
thread should finish up.  The thread notices this and cleans up and
exits. lli_sa_lock is used to avoid races between waking up the process
and the process exiting.

Change-Id: I0c807dfc3d9cc307e30b024e7a75c5d541fbaa12
Signed-off-by: NeilBrown <neilb@suse.com>
Signed-off-by: Dmitry Eremin <dmitry.eremin@intel.com>
